perturbation.original_text: The Proprietary Information will be marked by the Owner as confidential at the tim e of disclosure. I f Proprietar y Inform ation is disclosed orally , electroni cally or wit hout an y marking, Owner will identif y it as confidential at the time of disclosure and will pr ovide a written confirmation to Recipien t within thirty days of disclosure that such inform ation is confidentia l. The parties’ obli gations with respect to Pr oprietary Inform ation shall con tinue for a period of three (3) years after the term ination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Proprietary Information will be marked by the Owner as confidential at the time of disclosure. The parties’ obligations with respect to Proprietary Information shall continue for a period of three (3) years after the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the clause regarding oral or electronic disclosure and the subsequent written confirmation, the contract creates ambiguity regarding the protection of information disclosed without marking. This contradicts the overall intent of the agreement to protect proprietary information. If oral disclosures aren't followed up with written confirmation the Recipient can argue that the Proprietary Information is not being protected, creating an in-text contradiction on the responsabilities regarding Proprietary Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Propriet ary Information" will i nclude any and all techni cal, engineering, scientific, financial, marketing and/or competitive inform ation or data, including but not lim ited to form ulas, databases, schematics, circuit designs, cell libraries, processes, know-how, m ethodologies, i nventions, al gorithm , techni ques, ideas, software, docu mentation, trade secret s, projections, licenses, marketing and product deve lopment plans, and any ot her inform ation of a sim ilar nature whether disclosed orally or in wri ting, or through observation, exam inatio n or use, specifically : SOFTWARE: _____ ________ ___________ ___________ ___________ ___________ ___________ ___________ ____ Each party agrees to main tain the confidentiality of such Pr oprietary Inform ation and to not use, disclose, publish or dissem inate it in an y manner not consiste nt with this Agreement. In additi on to any other remedies, which may be available, Owner shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief to enforce the ter ms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the requirement for written confirmation of orally disclosed proprietary information. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which defines 'Proprietary Information' as including information disclosed orally and requires confidentiality to be maintained for all Proprietary Information. Without the written confirmation requirement, the Recipient can argue that oral disclosures are not being protected, creating a contradiction regarding the responsibilities regarding Proprietary Information as per the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: For Proprietary Information receiv ed from the disclosing party, the Recipient agrees to: a. hold it in confidence w ith the sa me degree of c are w ith which it protects its own confidential and proprietary inform ation, but at least re asonable care ; b. restrict dis closure of it only to its e mployees, agents and contractors with a need to know basis and advise thos e persons of their obligations to keep it confidential; c. except for the purposes of this Agre ement, not copy or duplicate, or knowingl y allow anyone else to copy or duplicate it; d. not publish, disclose or make availab le any benchmark or testing results without the written consent of both parties; and e. on req uest or up on term ination of this Agreem ent, prom ptly return all Proprietary Inform ation in a tangible form or certify that such Proprietary Inform ation is destroy ed.
perturbation.changed_text: For Proprietary Information receiv ed from the disclosing party, the Recipient agrees to: a. hold it in confidence w ith the sa me degree of c are w ith which it protects its own confidential and proprietary inform ation, but at least re asonable care ; b. restrict dis closure of it only to its e mployees, agents and contractors with a need to know basis and advise thos e persons of their obligations to keep it confidential; c. except for the purposes of this Agre ement, not copy or duplicate, or knowingl y allow anyone else to copy or duplicate it; and e. on req uest or up on term ination of this Agreem ent, prom ptly return all Proprietary Inform ation in a tangible form or certify that such Proprietary Inform ation is destroy ed.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the restriction 'not publish, disclose or make available any benchmark or testing results without the written consent of both parties,' there's a contradiction because there are no clauses that explicitly prohibit publishing benchmark results. This undermines the protection of sensitive information and contradicts the broader purpose of the agreement to maintain confidentiality, because even with a confidentiality agreement in place, benchmarks can be disclosed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Propriet ary Information" will i nclude any and all techni cal, engineering, scientific, financial, marketing and/or competitive inform ation or data, including but not lim ited to form ulas, databases, schematics, circuit designs, cell libraries, processes, know-how, m ethodologies, i nventions, al gorithm , techni ques, ideas, software, docu mentation, trade secret s, projections, licenses, marketing and product deve lopment plans, and any ot her inform ation of a sim ilar nature whether disclosed orally or in wri ting, or through observation, exam inatio n or use, specifically : SOFTWARE: _____ ________ ___________ ___________ ___________ ___________ ___________ ___________ ____ Each party agrees to main tain the confidentiality of such Pr oprietary Inform ation and to not use, disclose, publish or dissem inate it in an y manner not consiste nt with this Agreement. In additi on to any other remedies, which may be available, Owner shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief to enforce the ter ms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifically prohibited publishing benchmark results without consent. The changed text omits this restriction, directly contradicting the contradicted text, which states that proprietary information, including technical data, should not be disclosed in any manner inconsistent with the agreement. The omission allows for the potential disclosure of benchmark results, which falls under proprietary information, creating a direct conflict and thus, a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Obligatio ns of parties under this Ag reemen t shall not appl y to i nform ation which: a. was alread y in the publi c domain when disclosed; or sub sequent ly falls into t he public d omain other than throug h disclosure by the Recipient; or disclosed by the R ecipient with the written approval of the Owner; b. was alread y in the possession of or known by the Recipient free of any obligation at the time disclosed to Recipient; c. beco mes known to the Recipient from a source o ther than the Owner with out breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; d. is develope d independen tly by the Recipient withou t use of the Owner’s Propri etary Inform ation; or e. is disclosed p ursuant t o judicial or governm ental order, provided that t he Recipient notifies the Owner so that an adequate opp ortunit y is gi ven to respon d to such order.
perturbation.changed_text: Obligatio ns of parties under this Ag reemen t shall not appl y to i nform ation which: a. was alread y in the publi c domain when disclosed; or sub sequent ly falls into t he public d omain other than throug h disclosure by the Recipient; b. was alread y in the possession of or known by the Recipient free of any obligation at the time disclosed to Recipient; c. beco mes known to the Recipient from a source o ther than the Owner with out breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; d. is develope d independen tly by the Recipient withou t use of the Owner’s Propri etary Inform ation; or e. is disclosed p ursuant t o judicial or governm ental order.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'provided that the Recipient notifies the Owner so that an adequate opportunity is given to respond to such order', the recipient could disclose information pursuant to a governmental order without giving the owner a chance to legally challenge the disclosure. This makes the overall protection of the Proprietary Information weaker and inconsistent with the purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Propriet ary Information" will i nclude any and all techni cal, engineering, scientific, financial, marketing and/or competitive inform ation or data, including but not lim ited to form ulas, databases, schematics, circuit designs, cell libraries, processes, know-how, m ethodologies, i nventions, al gorithm , techni ques, ideas, software, docu mentation, trade secret s, projections, licenses, marketing and product deve lopment plans, and any ot her inform ation of a sim ilar nature whether disclosed orally or in wri ting, or through observation, exam inatio n or use, specifically : SOFTWARE: _____ ________ ___________ ___________ ___________ ___________ ___________ ___________ ____ Each party agrees to main tain the confidentiality of such Pr oprietary Inform ation and to not use, disclose, publish or dissem inate it in an y manner not consiste nt with this Agreement. In additi on to any other remedies, which may be available, Owner shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief to enforce the ter ms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the recipient to notify the owner of judicial or governmental orders to allow the owner to respond. The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text states that each party agrees to maintain confidentiality and not disclose information in a manner inconsistent with the agreement. By removing the notification requirement, the changed text allows the recipient to disclose proprietary information pursuant to a governmental order without giving the owner a chance to challenge it, which is inconsistent with the obligation to maintain confidentiality. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will bind both parties’ affiliates, subsid iaries, successors an d assigns. Recipient cer tifies that no Proprietary Inform ation, or any part of it will be exported to any countr y in violation of the export laws and regulations of the United States government. If any portion of this Agreem ent is deter mined to be invali d or unenfor ceable, th e remainder wi ll be in effect . Failure by either part y to en force any provision of this Agreem ent will not be deem ed a waiver of future enforcement of that or any other pr ovisi on. Any m odification s to this Agree ment must be made in writing and must be signed by both part ies. This Agreement w ill be governed by the laws of the State of California.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will bind both parties’ affiliates, subsid iaries, successors an d assigns. Recipient cer tifies that no Proprietary Inform ation, or any part of it will be exported to any countr y in violation of the export laws and regulations of the United States government. If any portion of this Agreem ent is deter mined to be invali d or unenfor ceable, th e remainder wi ll be in effect . Failure by either part y to en force any provision of this Agreem ent will not be deem ed a waiver of future enforcement of that or any other pr ovisi on. This Agreement w ill be governed by the laws of the State of California.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the sentence 'Any m odification s to this Agree ment must be made in writing and must be signed by both part ies,' the contract doesn't explicitly require written and signed modifications. This makes the agreement vulnerable to verbal agreements or unsigned documents being considered valid amendments, which directly conflicts with the intent to clearly define and protect proprietary information, as verbal agreements could easily be disputed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Propriet ary Information" will i nclude any and all techni cal, engineering, scientific, financial, marketing and/or competitive inform ation or data, including but not lim ited to form ulas, databases, schematics, circuit designs, cell libraries, processes, know-how, m ethodologies, i nventions, al gorithm , techni ques, ideas, software, docu mentation, trade secret s, projections, licenses, marketing and product deve lopment plans, and any ot her inform ation of a sim ilar nature whether disclosed orally or in wri ting, or through observation, exam inatio n or use, specifically : SOFTWARE: _____ ________ ___________ ___________ ___________ ___________ ___________ ___________ ____ Each party agrees to main tain the confidentiality of such Pr oprietary Inform ation and to not use, disclose, publish or dissem inate it in an y manner not consiste nt with this Agreement. In additi on to any other remedies, which may be available, Owner shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief to enforce the ter ms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text required modifications to be written and signed. The changed text omits this requirement. The contradicted text defines 'Proprietary Information' broadly, including information disclosed orally. Without the requirement for written modifications, verbal agreements could alter the scope or terms related to this proprietary information, creating a contradiction. The original text had protection against that, but the Changed Text doesn't have the protection of having it in writing.
